items.title: Merck continues New Jersey cuts, laying off 88 in Rahway
items.snippet: Follow along as BioSpace tracks job cuts and restructuring initiatives.

items.title: ASCO: Pfizer scores in lung, colorectal and prostate cancer but key readouts still to come
items.snippet: Pfizer showcased multiple late-breakers at the American Society of Clinical Oncology’s annual conference but its biggest data are expected later this year.

items.title: ASCO: Merck overshadowed by upstart biotechs but TROP2 plans begin to pay off
items.snippet: Merck may not have had the splashiest presentation at the American Society of Clinical Oncology meeting, but the data show why the Big Pharma remains king of oncology, analysts say.

items.title: Fulcrum tanks as cancer concerns stymie sickle cell asset, trigger business reorg
items.snippet: Fulcrum Therapeutics’ is down more than 50% Tuesday morning as the biotech discontinues development of its lead asset. Ipsen’s Tazverik, which belongs to the same class, was pulled from the market in March due to the risk of secondary malignancies.

items.title: FDA moves away from animal testing for cancer therapeutics
items.snippet: A year of significant policy change at the FDA brought momentum and scrutiny into the new year. As 2026 gets underway, biopharma companies are responding to sweeping vaccine changes while concerns surface about the politicization of the agency.

items.title: Lilly bets $3B+ to add China’s Haisco to growing list of partners in five-program pact
items.snippet: Eli Lilly and the Haisco Pharmaceutical Group have yet to disclose what specific indications they plan to prioritize.

items.title: ASCO: Moderna’s mRNA-based melanoma vaccine shows ‘encouraging’ 5-year survival
items.snippet: When used alongside Merck’s Keytruda, Moderna’s personalized cancer vaccine halved the risk of death or disease recurrence in a mid-stage trial of advanced melanoma—a result analysts said could help “instill confidence” in the regimen.

items.title: Big Pharma makes ‘creative effort’ to fold Chinese assets into pipelines
items.snippet: Recent deals from Pfizer and Bristol Myers Squibb hint at Big Pharma’s more collaborative approach to partnering with Chinese companies.

items.title: France’s biotech ambition is strong but its scale-up engine still needs work
items.snippet: Government backing, deep scientific talent and a robust pharma heritage are helping France punch above its weight, but turning research into investable companies remains a challenge.

items.title: BrainStorm levels up, bringing on former FDA leader Peter Pitts for new ALS bid
items.snippet: For Peter Pitts, a former associate commissioner at the FDA, the appointment to the board of BrainStorm Cell Therapeutics is an opportunity to fulfill a promise he made long ago to a patient with ALS.

items.title: ASCO: A battle of bispecifics, Eli Lilly’s big win and more
items.snippet: Practice-changing data in lung cancer, prostate cancer and more were on display over the weekend at the American Society of Clinical Oncology annual meeting in Chicago. Plus, early readouts on assets that could reshape the cancer landscape.

items.title: 17 companies expected to create 400+ Massachusetts jobs add under 50
items.snippet: Seventeen life sciences businesses awarded Massachusetts tax incentives to add and retain 426 combined jobs hit 10% of that target as of Dec. 31. Three companies, including Novartis, had reported layoffs either last year or this year.

items.title: Servier frees Edgewise for cardio mission with $2.65B muscular dystrophy deal
items.snippet: Edgewise Therapeutics will now focus on a handful of cardiovascular programs including EDG-7500 for hypertrophic cardiomyopathy thanks to the non-dilutive capital from France’s Servier.

items.title: CordenPharma inks AmbioPharm buyout to continue peptide production expansion
items.snippet: Swiss service provider CordenPharma is acquiring AmbioPharm, gaining facilities such as a plant in South Carolina to provide a fully U.S.-based supply.

items.title: ASCO: Summit reached with China lung data but new climb begins to prove global benefit
items.snippet: “King Keytruda's reign continues,” analysts at BMO Capital Markets declared after Chinese data for Summit Therapeutics’ ivonescimab were revealed at the American Society for Clinical Oncology in Chicago.

items.title: Moderna partners with CEPI on Ebola vaccine efforts amid outbreak in Africa
items.snippet: Riding a newfound surge of interest on infectious disease concerns, Moderna is linking up with a global vaccine foundation in hopes of developing a vaccine to slow the new surge in Ebola underway in the Congo and Uganda.

items.title: Lilly maintains deal streak with $1.2B pact for Hanmi's GLP-2 candidate
items.snippet: Expanding in the metabolic space, Eli Lilly has struck a back-loaded licensing deal with South Korea’s Hanmi Pharm for a mid-stage GLP-2 agonist being trialed for short bowel syndrome.

items.title: ASCO: Biopharma has pancreatic cancer ‘surrounded’ as Immuneering’s drug adds 9 quality months
items.snippet: In addition to eliciting 17.3 months median overall survival vs. 8.5 months for patients given standard of care treatment in a prior study, Immuneering’s atebimetinib demonstrated a robust tolerability profile—something CEO Ben Zeskind said equates highly with survival.

items.title: ASCO: Kelun-Merck ADC shines with differentiated overall survival in first-line lung cancer
items.snippet: Interim overall survival data on a TROP2 ADC from Merck and Chinese partner Kelun-Biotech provide support for the pharma’s big bet on its potential to help navigate Keytruda’s impending loss of exclusivity.

items.title: ASCO: Revolution poised to capture RAS space as pancreatic cancer dataset ‘derisks’ approval
items.snippet: Revolution Medicines and its RAS inhibitor daraxonrasib stole the show at the American Society of Clinical Oncology’s annual meeting this weekend, as Truist Securities predicts a possible third quarter launch for the pancreatic cancer drug.

items.title: Lilly just bought renowned regulator Peter Marks a pipeline to play with
items.snippet: FDA veteran Peter Marks will now shape the future of Eli Lilly’s vaccines work after the buys of Curevo, LimmaTech Biologics and Vaccine Company for up to $3.8 billion total.

items.title: 3 top FDA, CDC roles filled by 15 different people in less than 18 months
items.snippet: The Department of Health and Human Services is spinning its wheels, unable to establish steady leadership at three major divisions—the CDC and the FDA’s two primary review units.

items.title: Unforced errors flummox HHS’ radical transparency bid—again
items.snippet: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s health department has consistently touted radical transparency as being key to its mission. Recent instances—the FDA’s decision not to disclose the recipients of three Commissioner’s National Priority Vouchers and FDA and CDC choices not to publish vaccine-related papers—call this intent into q...

items.title: BioSpace elevates journalism as biopharma evolves in the Trump era
items.snippet: Neal and Azbee awards have validated our approach to reporting on the industry at a time of unprecedented shifts at the FDA and other federal agencies.
